text: CEASEFIRE EXTENDED: Trump announced 3-week extension of Israel-Lebanon ceasefire after 2nd round of White House talks. Original truce set to expire Sunday April 26 — now extended through mid-May. Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun invited to Washington. Rubio credits Trump personally for making it possible.
verificationNote: Washington Post, CNBC, CBS News. All confirmed.

text: HEZBOLLAH ROCKETS: ~4 rockets fired at Shtula, northern Israel — first rocket attack on Israeli territory during the ceasefire. All intercepted. Hezbollah says response to Israeli shelling of Yater. IDF destroyed launcher. Separately, Israeli airstrike on Shoukine road killed 3, Yater shelling wounded 2 incl. child.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Israel National News, Naharnet, Middle East Eye. All confirmed.

text: PENTAGON COVER-UP: Intercept reports 15 wounded-in-action troops quietly erased from Iran war casualty count — total dropped from 428 to 413 overnight with zero explanation. Pentagon spokespersons can't account for the change. US gov official: 'the definition of a cover-up.' True WIA figure likely 428+.
verificationNote: The Intercept investigative report confirmed. Military Times data discrepancy tracked.

text: HORMUZ MULTINATIONAL: 30+ nations held 2-day conference at UK Permanent Joint HQ, Northwood to plan multinational Strait of Hormuz reopening. Led by UK and France. Mine clearance, command structure, deployment planning discussed. This is the most concrete multinational military response to the blockade yet.
verificationNote: GOV.UK official announcement, Jerusalem Post, Washington Examiner. All confirmed.

text: DAY 56 ROUNDUP: Lebanon ceasefire extended 3 weeks. Hezbollah rockets at Shtula intercepted. Israeli strikes kill 3 in Shoukine. Two IDF soldiers indicted for Iran espionage. Pentagon removes 15 wounded. 30+ nations plan Hormuz reopening. EU widens sanctions on 19 officials. CSIS: 50% Patriot interceptors expended. Brent $103.38. Gas $4.03.
verificationNote: WaPo, Haaretz, Intercept, GOV.UK, Al Jazeera, CSIS, Al Arabiya, Fortune, AAA. All verified.

text: OIL BREAKING: Brent crude breaches $105 ($105.73) for first time since April 8 ceasefire. 32.5% above pre-war baseline. WTI $96.17 (+27.4%). Driven by Iran ship seizures, Trump 'shoot and kill' order, Kharg Island near storage capacity (Bessent: 'full in days'). IEA Birol: 'biggest energy security threat in history' — 13M bbl/day lost.
verificationNote: Oneindia, CNBC, Fortune, IEA confirmed. Brent $105.73 verified across multiple exchanges.

text: CARRIER UPDATE: USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) confirmed in CENTCOM AOR — 3rd US carrier alongside Ford and Lincoln. Largest US naval aviation concentration in the region in decades. CENTCOM blockade: 10,000+ troops, 17 warships, 100+ aircraft. 33+ vessels redirected. M/T Majestic X seized in Indian Ocean = 3rd tanker captured.
verificationNote: WaPo, India TV News, Military.com, Stars and Stripes. All confirmed.

text: KATZ ESCALATION: Israeli Defence Min declares IDF 'ready to return Iran to the Stone Age' — awaiting US green light. Targets: energy grid, Khamenei dynasty, electricity infrastructure. Comes same day Brent crosses $105 and Senate defeats 5th War Powers vote 46-51. Only Rand Paul (R) crossed. May 1 deadline looms.
verificationNote: Euronews, Antiwar.com, NBC, WION. Democracy Now, Foreign Policy on Senate vote.

text: NATO CRISIS: Pentagon memo floats suspending Spain from NATO over Iran war refusal, reviewing UK Falklands position. 'They were not there for us.' Meanwhile Trump demands Iran cut Hezbollah funding: 'That is a must.' Rubio says Iranian officials 'saying the right things in private.' Pope Leo: 'I cannot be in favor of war.'
verificationNote: Al-Monitor/ToI on NATO memo. ToI/CNN on Trump Hezbollah. The Hill on Rubio. America Magazine on Pope.

text: HUMANITARIAN IMPACT: UNDP warns 30M+ pushed back into poverty across 162 countries from Iran war energy/food shocks. FDD estimates $91B replacement cost to Iran from Operation Epic Fury (Iran self-assesses $300B-$1T). IMF cuts global growth to 3.1%. Iran GDP -6.1%. Rebuilding could take 'more than a decade.'
verificationNote: UNDP.org, Al Jazeera, FDD.org, IMF WEO April 2026. All verified.

text: DAY 56 LATE ROUNDUP: Brent $105.73 (+32.5%). USS Bush = 3rd carrier. Katz: 'Stone Age.' Pentagon NATO memo targets Spain. Senate War Powers defeated 46-51. M/T Majestic X seized (3rd tanker). IEA: 'biggest energy crisis in history.' UNDP: 30M+ into poverty. Trump: Iran must cut Hezbollah. FDD: $91B damage. Navy Sec fired. Gas $4.03.
verificationNote: CNBC, WaPo, Euronews, Al-Monitor, Democracy Now, Fortune, UNDP.org, ToI, FDD, CNN, AAA. All verified.

text: HORMUZ CRITICAL: Only 5 ships transited Strait of Hormuz in past 24 hours — including just 1 Iranian oil tanker. Down from 7 ships yesterday. Pre-war: 138/day. That's a 96% decline. 800+ vessels stranded in Gulf. 40,000+ seafarers trapped. War-risk insurance now 2-6% of hull value (vs 0.15-0.25% pre-war). Brent $107.14.
verificationNote: US News, CNBC, WEF (insurance data). Al Jazeera confirmed oil prices.

text: LEBANON FRONT: IDF drone shot down over Tyre by Hezbollah anti-aircraft missile — IDF says 'no fear of information leak.' IDF kills Hezbollah operative between Kounine and Beit Yahoun in drone strike — first targeting of operatives since ceasefire. IDF orders evacuation of Deir Aames. Hezbollah drone intercepted over Western Galilee. Ceasefire extended 3 weeks but combat continues.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, JNS, Ya Libnan, Xinhua, Ynet. IDF official statements confirmed.

text: NETANYAHU CANCER: Israeli PM reveals he secretly underwent prostate cancer treatment during the war — radiation therapy ~2.5 months ago. Now declared cancer-free. He delayed disclosure to prevent Iranian propaganda. Questions about leadership capacity during wartime treated as secondary to his recovery announcement.
verificationNote: CNN, Haaretz, Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel. Official Israeli medical report.

text: ROUND 2 TALKS? Iran FM Araghchi heading to Islamabad Friday night for potential second round of US-Iran peace talks. US logistics team already in place. Pakistan mediating. Iran: 'We are all Iranian and revolutionary' — coordinated unity statement from Pezeshkian, Araghchi, Qalibaf rejecting Trump's claims of internal divisions. Guterres welcomes ceasefire extension.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Gulf News, Pakistan MOFA, WTOP, Middle East Monitor, UN News. All confirmed.

text: MARKETS & SANCTIONS: OFAC sanctioned 14 entities in Iran, Turkey, UAE for missile/UAV procurement — Turkish company Emti Fiber sanctioned for nitrocellulose. Brent $107.14 (+34.3%). S&P 500 7,108 (-0.41%). Iranian rial 1,554,000/USD (black market). Wheat surging — KC wheat +29¢ on Kansas drought/freeze damage. USDA: 3.1% food price rise in 2026.
verificationNote: Treasury.gov, CNBC, TheStreet, Bonbast, Grain Central, Investigate Midwest. All verified.

text: DAY 56 MORNING ROUNDUP: Brent $107.14 (+34.3%). WTI $97.40 (+29.0%). Only 5 ships transit Hormuz. IDF drone shot down over Tyre. IDF kills Hezbollah operative near Kounine. Netanyahu cancer-free. Araghchi heading to Islamabad. Hegseth: blockade 'going global.' OFAC sanctions 14. Gas $4.031. EU widens sanctions. Iran unity statement.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, US News, ToI, CNN, Bloomberg, ABC News, Treasury.gov, AAA. All verified.

text: LEBANON CEASEFIRE VIOLATION: Israeli warplanes struck a house in Touline (Marjayoun district) at dawn April 24 — 2 killed per Lebanese Health Ministry. Artillery shelling followed. Strike came HOURS after Trump announced 3-week ceasefire extension at the White House. At least 9 killed in south Lebanon since April 17 ceasefire. UN: Israel's strikes 'may breach international humanitarian law.'
verificationNote: CBC News, Al Jazeera liveblog, US News. UN statement confirmed by OHCHR spokesperson.

text: OIL PULLBACK: Brent crude pulls back to $104.63 (+31.0% from pre-war $79.84) from intraday high of $106.80. WTI $93.90 (-2% on session, +24.4% from pre-war). Markets pricing in Witkoff/Kushner Saturday flight to Islamabad for direct Round 2 talks with Araghchi. Vance on standby. Gas $4.05 AAA (-6¢ WoW).
verificationNote: CNBC, Trading Economics, AAA Newsroom, Axios, CBS News. All verified.

text: HEGSETH PENTAGON: Calls US war with Iran 'a gift to the world.' 'We are not counting on Europe, but they need the Strait of Hormuz much more than we do.' 'Iran has a historic chance to make a serious deal, and the ball is in their court.' Announces second aircraft carrier joining blockade; 'blockade is growing and going global.' Called on Iran to 'abandon a nuclear weapon in meaningful and verifiable ways.'
verificationNote: PBS, Stars and Stripes, C-SPAN official transcript. All verified.

text: VATICAN & EU: Pope Leo XIV — 'as a pastor, I cannot support the US-Israeli war with Iran' (though condemns Iran's killing of protesters). Echoes April 11 'delusion of omnipotence' critique. EU High Rep Kallas warns nuclear experts must join Iran talks or agreement will be 'weaker than JCPOA' and lead to 'a more dangerous Iran.' UK PM Starmer pledges to proscribe IRGC as terrorist organization by July 2026.
verificationNote: CNN, NPR on Pope Leo. The National, EEAS on Kallas. Jewish Chronicle on Starmer.

text: DAY 56 MIDDAY ROUNDUP: Brent pulls back to $104.63 (-$2.51 from morning high) on Witkoff/Kushner Saturday Pakistan talks. WTI $93.90. Gas $4.05 AAA (-6¢ WoW). Touline airstrike kills 2 in south Lebanon despite ceasefire. Hegseth: Iran war 'a gift to the world.' Pope Leo 'cannot support' war as pastor. Kallas: nuclear experts must join talks. Starmer pledges IRGC terror proscription by July.
verificationNote: CNBC, CBC, PBS, CNN, The National, Jewish Chronicle, AAA, Axios. All verified.
